_comment: ZeroBreach permission / integrity / privilege-escalation baseline. Externalized from ZeroBreach-V23.ps1 so the engine body holds no path/signature literals (AMSI-safe; data files are not AMSI-scanned). Consumed by the FORENSIC PERMISSION & INTEGRITY AUDIT phases (108-115). All values are filesystem paths, registry keys, account names, and service names — NOT malware signatures. Edit here without touching the engine.
_acl_note: critical_acl_paths: directories/files that must NEVER be writable by Everyone / BUILTIN\Users / Authenticated Users. A write/modify/fullcontrol Allow ACE for those identities on these paths = privilege-escalation surface (an attacker can replace a SYSTEM-run binary).
_protected_files_note: protected_system_files: high-value binaries frequently targeted for replacement/hijack or used as auto-elevated LOLBins. Integrity phase verifies Authenticode + catalog signature and flags any that are unsigned, invalid, or signed by a non-Microsoft publisher (a Microsoft-named file with a 3rd-party / absent signature is a strong tamper indicator).
_accessibility_note: accessibility_binaries: the classic 'sticky keys / utilman' backdoor set — if any of these is replaced (bad signature) or has its IFEO debugger set, an attacker gets a SYSTEM shell from the logon screen. Integrity + IFEO cross-check.
_reg_acl_note: critical_reg_acl_keys: registry keys whose ACL should only allow SYSTEM / Administrators / TrustedInstaller to write. A write Allow ACE for Everyone / Users / Authenticated Users here = persistence/privesc surface.
_weak_identity_note: weak_write_identities: SID-resolvable account names that should never hold write/modify/fullcontrol on the protected paths/keys above. Matched case-insensitively as a substring of IdentityReference.
_trusted_owners_note: trusted_file_owners: acceptable owners for protected system files. Anything else owning a System32 binary is suspicious (ownership change is a common tamper step).
_trusted_publishers_note: trusted_signers: publisher Subject substrings considered legitimate for OS / mainstream-vendor binaries. Used to flag Microsoft-named files NOT signed by Microsoft, and to lower severity on validly-signed mainstream software.
_service_note: privesc_safe_service_publishers mirrors trusted_signers for service-binary checks. unquoted_path_whitelist: substrings of ImagePath that are known-benign even when unquoted with spaces (rare; keep tight).
_path_note: system_path_extra_dirs: directories that, if writable by a non-admin AND present on the machine/system PATH, create a DLL/binary planting surface. The phase also enumerates the live machine PATH dynamically; this list seeds well-known high-risk locations.
_health_note: expected_running_services: security-relevant services that should be Running on a healthy, protected endpoint. A stopped/disabled one is a strong tamper indicator after an infection. expected_security_values: registry settings that, if changed, indicate protection was disabled.
_defender_tamper_note: defender_tamper_values: HKLM policy values that disable Defender. value present and set to the listed 'bad' number = tampered.
